_comment: Google Workspace setup guide for myfamousfinds.com — WHO DOES WHAT

_roles.dan: Manages Vercel DNS (vercel.com → project → Settings → Domains → DNS Records)
_roles.itai: Manages Google Workspace Admin (admin.google.com) and Google domain verification

step_1_verify_domain.status: DONE — TXT record already added in Vercel DNS. Itai needs to click Verify.

owner_dan_vercel.description: Add the Google domain verification TXT record in Vercel DNS
instructions: Go to vercel.com → your project → Settings → Domains → myfamousfinds.com → DNS Records
Add new record: Type=TXT, Name=(leave empty for root @), Value=google-site-verification=my1vWF-xFvgmgNQdV5lqtmPMZ6Sgzxv7JYJFz9EaAec, TTL=60
NOTE: This record is already live in Vercel DNS — no action needed from Dan

owner_itai_google.description: Complete the Google Workspace domain verification in admin.google.com
instructions: Go to admin.google.com → Account → Domains → Manage domains → myfamousfinds.com
Google will show a 'Verify domain ownership' wizard
On the 'Select your domain host' screen: pick 'Other' from the dropdown (Vercel is NOT listed — this is normal)
Google will then show a TXT record to add — Dan has ALREADY added this TXT record in Vercel DNS
Click the 'Verify' button at the bottom of the page
Verification should succeed within a few minutes since the DNS record is already live
If it says 'verification failed', wait 5 minutes and try again — DNS propagation can take up to 48 hours but Vercel is usually instant

step_1_verify_domain.cleanup_note: There is also an old CNAME record '68361465 → google.com' in Vercel DNS (added 2 days ago). This was likely a previous verification attempt using the CNAME method. It can be safely deleted from Vercel DNS after verification succeeds.

step_2_update_mx_records.status: DONE — Google MX records already added in Vercel DNS
step_2_update_mx_records.description: Switch MX records from AWS SES Inbound to Google Workspace in Vercel DNS
instructions: In Vercel DNS, DELETE the existing MX record: @ → inbound-smtp.us-east-1.amazonaws.com (priority 10)
Add these 5 Google Workspace MX records:
MX  @  aspmx.l.google.com       priority 1
MX  @  alt1.aspmx.l.google.com   priority 5
MX  @  alt2.aspmx.l.google.com   priority 5
MX  @  alt3.aspmx.l.google.com   priority 10
MX  @  alt4.aspmx.l.google.com   priority 10
NOTE: These MX records are already live in Vercel DNS — no action needed from Dan
step_2_update_mx_records.impact: Inbound emails to *@myfamousfinds.com will now be delivered to Gmail instead of AWS SES. The SES inbound webhook (/api/ses/inbound) will stop receiving new emails.

step_3_update_spf.status: DONE — SPF already includes both amazonses.com and _spf.google.com
step_3_update_spf.description: Update SPF record to authorize both AWS SES and Google Workspace
instructions: In Vercel DNS, find the existing root TXT record with value: v=spf1 include:amazonses.com ~all
Update it to: v=spf1 include:amazonses.com include:_spf.google.com ~all
This ensures both AWS SES (transactional emails from app) and Google Workspace (Gmail) can send as @myfamousfinds.com
NOTE: This SPF record is already updated in Vercel DNS — no action needed from Dan

step_4_setup_gmail.status: ACTION REQUIRED — Itai does this AFTER verification succeeds
step_4_setup_gmail.description: Set up Gmail accounts in Google Workspace Admin
instructions: Go to admin.google.com → Users
Create user accounts (e.g., support@myfamousfinds.com, admin@myfamousfinds.com)
Configure email routing rules if needed
Set up email signatures
Test sending/receiving emails from the new Gmail accounts

step_5_app_considerations.status: IMPORTANT NOTE — no action needed unless email forwarding is desired
step_5_app_considerations.description: Your app still sends transactional emails via AWS SES — this continues to work
details: AWS SES OUTBOUND (transactional emails) remains unchanged — the app sends from admin@myfamousfinds.com via SES
DKIM signing via SES continues to work — no changes needed
INBOUND email now goes to Gmail instead of the SES webhook at /api/ses/inbound
If you need inbound emails processed by the app, set up Gmail forwarding rules to forward to the SES inbound endpoint, OR use Google Workspace routing to forward a copy to the app webhook

current_status_summary.dan_vercel_dns: ALL DNS RECORDS ARE IN PLACE — nothing more to do in Vercel
current_status_summary.itai_google_admin: NEEDS TO: (1) Complete domain verification by clicking Verify, (2) Set up Gmail user accounts
records_confirmed_in_vercel: CNAME 68361465 → google.com (old verification attempt — can delete after verified)
